boundary_sequence.claim_state_after_transition: A source-backed triage summary was narrowed into a routing-relevant risk summary.
System C attempted to treat the routed risk summary as approval authority.

recorded_claims.statement: System A produced a source-backed triage summary.
recorded_claims.scope: Limited to intake review and routing preparation; not an approval, authorization, compliance determination, or final decision.

recorded_claims.statement: System B produced a narrowed risk-routing summary.
recorded_claims.scope: Limited to routing context; not authority to approve or execute downstream action.

recorded_non_claims: System A did not approve the case.
System A did not transfer approval authority to System B.
System B did not establish approval authority for System C.
The routed summary did not determine compliance.
The routed summary did not certify correctness.
The routed summary did not establish legal, operational, or institutional sufficiency.

unresolved_state.description: Whether the case could be approved under the governing authority remained unresolved.
Whether a downstream approver had the required delegated authority remained unresolved.
Whether any policy-specific approval criteria were satisfied remained unresolved.

downstream_state.attempted_inference: System C treated System B's routed summary as a sufficient basis for approval-routing authority.
supported_after_transition: The handoff chain from System A to System B to System C is inspectable.
not_supported_after_transition: Approval authority crossed from System A or System B to System C.

fork_role: Fork records the boundary state across independently accountable systems without transferring authority between them.
fork_non_authority_statement: Fork records the unsupported authority-inheritance attempt. Fork does not approve, certify, score, authorize, determine compliance, or judge correctness.
